headline: Orcadian advances Earlham & Orwell gas-to-wire concept with integrated carbon capture and reinjection
summary: Orcadian Energy commenced the Assessment Phase for its 100%-owned UK North Sea licence P2680, with a preferred concept of a ~200 MW offshore gas-fired power station with integrated CO2 capture and reinjection into the Earlham reservoir, powering a co-located offshore data centre (the 'Earlham Gigagrid'). The high CO2 content of Earlham gas makes capture-and-reinject the preferred monetisation route; progress is subject to NSTA licence assignment and a Letter of No Objection.
co2crc_note: A novel gas-to-wire + capture-and-reinject model tied to powering data-centre load — a reservoir-reinjection concept relevant to Australian high-CO2 gas developments.
extractor_note: Early-stage development concept / assessment phase; ~200 MW is power capacity, not CO2 Mtpa, so capacity_mtpa null. The GBP 1.34m referenced is a debt repayment, not a CCS commitment, so amount null.

headline: Industry groups urge Baltic states to clear the Helsinki Convention barrier to offshore CO2 storage
summary: A coalition of industry stakeholders called on Baltic-bordering governments to remove obstacles to offshore CO2 storage. Unlike OSPAR and the London Protocol, the Helsinki Convention still legally bars sub-seabed CO2 storage in the Baltic; an independent review of the legality, environmental impact and technical feasibility of Baltic CCS is being sought.
co2crc_note: A regional-sea-convention barrier to sub-seabed CO2 storage directly parallels Australia's London Protocol / Sea Dumping Act pathway for Bayu-Undan-type transboundary storage.
extractor_note: Advocacy/regulatory-barrier item, no money dimension (commitment_status na). Specific industry bodies not named in the accessible text, so organisations left empty.

headline: Building materials made from sewage sludge shown to lock away CO2 for centuries
summary: Carbon Herald reports a CO2-mineralisation/utilisation approach that turns sewage sludge into building materials which durably sequester carbon — a niche but novel utilisation pathway that stores CO2 in the built environment.
co2crc_note: Peripheral CO2-utilisation pathway; limited direct relevance to CO2CRC's geological-storage focus.
extractor_note: Summarised from the Carbon Herald headline/trade coverage; research origin/geography not confirmed, region set to Global.

headline: Entergy and MHI Group sign MOU targeting a 50% cost cut for integrated gas-turbine CCS in the US
summary: US utility Entergy and Mitsubishi Heavy Industries (MHI America and Mitsubishi Power Americas) signed a memorandum of understanding to develop a near-term roadmap to halve the overall cost of integrated GTCC-plus-CCS, via a standardised approach. Entergy sits near the largest existing US CO2 pipeline network and favourable Gulf Coast storage geology.
co2crc_note: Cost-down roadmaps for gas-turbine CCS bear directly on the economics of gas-fired-power CCS that Australia would need for any post-combustion power deployment.
extractor_note: MOU (intention, no capital committed) -> commitment_status announced; MHI treated as diversified industrial (org_type other).

headline: IOGP Europe: 'targets don't build projects' — Europe needs enabling measures, not just CO2 storage targets
summary: In Carbon Herald coverage, IOGP Europe's Caterina De Matteis argued that Europe's CCS ambition risks outpacing delivery without commercially viable frameworks that connect emitters, transport operators and storage developers — reflecting industry discourse ahead of the EU's CO2 markets and infrastructure legislative proposal.
co2crc_note: A European policy-discourse signal on the gap between storage targets and bankable business cases.
extractor_note: Commentary/industry-body positioning (no instrument fits cleanly -> other); placed in media/social-licence section.
